original: \n\nYou’re immobilized! You can call for Emergency Help via your Codex, under the Extra Functions menu.
Alternatively, you can try to find something from your inventory you can use that might be able to help.

and dress your injuries with some less-than-legal nanogel you appropriated on an old job

You ring Paige up on your ship’s intercom. “Paige to the bridge,” you say into the receiver, “Paige to the bridge.”
\n\nYour Ausar navigator is by your side in just another moment. “Yes, captain?” she asks cheerfully.
\n\nYou tell her that you’re setting off, and you want your navigator to plot you a course. “Of course!” she says, excited to get to work. “Where are we headed?”
Where do you want to go?

You land on Phaedra II and disembark once more.
You find yourself in the "+e+"’s shower room.
\n\nNext to the shower is a medicine cabinet with various hygiene products.

There are a number of stalls in the shower room; the closest one is reserved the ship’s captain and the rest of your crew always leaves it open. That said, one of the other stalls is occupied, and you can hear the gentle singing of your Ausar lover and yoga instructor, Paige, over the running water.

\n\nFinally you feel you’ve gotten all the relaxation you can and shut off the water, stepping out and toweling yourself off. You slip your gear on with a refreshed smile, squeaky clean and ready to resume your adventure.
You open the medicine cabinet and find a small collection of body hygiene and toiletry supplies.
Feeling a little turned on, you decide that maybe you should have some fun shower sex with one of your crew. Who do you approach?

You pull the cabinet mirror to the side and take a good look at your [pc.beard].
\n\nUnfortunately, you don’t have the supplies to manipulate your living facial hair. You’ll have to visit a specialist if you want to change it in style and length.

\n\nWith the supplies that you have on hand, you guess you can’t do more than a shave. If you want to do anything fancier, you’ll probably have better luck visiting a stylist who specializes in fixing facial hair.
To prepare, you lather your lower face and around your [pc.lipsChaste], making sure to get the parts covered by your facial hair. You then wipe your hands dry and pull out
a cheap wireless electric razor. Flicking a switch, the battery charges and the device hums to life.
\n\nYou spend the next ten minutes or so shaving the [pc.beardNoun] from your visage. After you you’ve got it all, you turn on the sink faucet and rinse your face and slap on some aftershave.

\n\nTaking a final look at the mirror shows that the razor could only lop off so much, so you are left with some stubble decorating your face. If you want to remove it all, you’ll need to shave again.
an inexpensive, disposable razor. Getting in close, you slide the blade across the stubble on your face.

\n\nIt doesn’t take long, but you do find yourself having to repeat a few times in order to get rid of the rough spots. Once complete, you rub on some post-shave balm and feel the tingly, stinging sensation it leaves behind.
\n\nThe pain is worth it though. As you take a final review of your efforts, you grin. A clean shave. You no longer have a beard!

\n\nAfter some time, you finish browsing the articles and ogling the images, turn off the tablet, and set it aside.

residing in "+y+" of your "+p+" available crew space"+(1===p?"":"s")

\n\nYour ship is overloaded. Clear out some space in your ship’s storage before attempting to fly.
\n\nNo sense leaving while Paige is still in surgery...

Replacing the damaged component proves easier than you could have thought. You can fly your ship once more! Do you head to Mhen’ga to catch up to your cousin or swing back to Tavros for a bit of shopping?
\n\nWith a blown plasma conduit, you won’t be flying anywhere! You need to scavenge one from the station to progress!
You meticulously make your way back to the ship using every ounce of subtlety you possess.
Climbing back up the airlock, you
Having to take your time and almost getting caught sneaking around, you finally
It takes way longer than you would have thought thanks to a couple of near-misses, but you
make it safe and sound to the interior of your craft.

Deciding to let consumerism solve your problem, you flip open your codex and search for a speedy clothing drop service. When you find one, you request an emergency drop and send them your current coordinates.
\n\nYou don’t need to wait long as within a few minutes a service drone hovers toward your location with a supply box filled with mystery clothes.
You open your airlock and allow the drone to enter your ship.
It drops the box in front of you as a yellow light near its sensors blink awaitingly. It obviously expects a payment.
\n\nYou wave your codex over the drone’s sensors and the light turns
red. Your credit account is dry at the moment, of course, so this seems more like a charity drop than a business transaction. The drone makes a beeping tone of pity

orange. You didn’t have enough to pay the full price, but your Codex shows that the service took whatever you had left in your credit account. Well, this better be worth the cost... The drone makes a disappointed beeping noise
green. Your Codex shows that the service took "+e+" credits from your credit account. Well, this better be worth the cost... The drone makes a satisfied beeping melody

\n\nWith your new package in hand, you open the box and take out the vacuum-sealed contents inside. After opening each packet, you look at your new clothing items. Not an impressive spread, but at least it will keep you covered.
You collapse unconscious... and do not rise again, your body’s capacity to take damage reduced to less than nothing. Whoops.

You are currently wearing Sera’s Black Lace Collar.

\n\nThis collar is composed of soft, rich leather around its ring, out of which wings of black lace sprout, contouring with the shape of your neck. Tiny pearls dangle from the lace, making small clicking sounds when you move energetically. A large brass ring connects to the back; a strange, blue gemstone is affixed to the front, glowing softly from within. ‘PROPERTY OF SERA’ is embossed into the leather. You flip out your codex to check up on your biometric statistics. Noticing a small glitch with your body measurements, you tap a couple options to verify the data. With a few quick beeps, your codex prints out the following:
\n\nThe codex beeps once again to signify that the statistics have been updated. Your measurements should now be fixed.

\nRacial scores are not intended to be a full and true depiction of the transformatives that your character has consumed, but a peek behind the curtain of the system working as it should. In many instances it is not intended that you will be able to ‘fully score’ a race and have zero influence from any others. Interpret the percentages more as a “grade” for each race category rather than part of the character’s total racial composition.
